title: braya humilis subsp. arctica
intro: Braya humilis subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic Bunchberry or Alpine Bunchberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae), native to Arctic regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its rich content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Braya humilis subsp. arctica include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in treating various health conditions, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
